content: Just as sigmoid activation squashes unbounded input activations into a smooth 0-1 range to prioritize high-confidence signals and gate noisy low-magnitude inputs in a neural network, a central meta agent in parallel reader agent orchestration can use a sigmoid gating function to dynamically weight and aggregate partial outputs from parallel worker readers, dropping low-confidence contradictory ingestions and prioritizing high-consistency, high-relevance chunks for final synthesis.
source_topics: Parallel Orchestration & Ingestion with Reader Agents
trigger_topic: Parallel Orchestration & Ingestion with Reader Agents
